PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: VIE dark rites
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a severe communal rite - write awe, dread, and complicity from the pawn's assigned place, using only the ceremony and outcome supplied

instructions.li: a dark rite - stay close to what the pawn saw, smelled, heard, or helped do; let complicity carry more weight than explanation
a dark rite - write the crowd's shared purpose against the pawn's private fear, conviction, revulsion, or numbness
a dark rite - keep death, plague, fire, or forced conversion consequential and human; never turn suffering into spectacle for its own sake

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with ritual dread, public conviction, and private consequence

tones.li: awed and complicit, the ceremony larger than any one conscience
dark and bodily, public certainty pressing against private fear

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: VIE festivals and rites
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a communal rite of celebration, politics, devotion, or release - write the pawn's role and what the colony felt like in the middle of it

instructions.li: a communal rite - let the supplied title choose the register: warm festival, solemn hymn, political challenge, fair, conversion, or emancipation
a communal rite - write the pawn's place among the crowd and one concrete detail that made the colony feel briefly different
a communal rite - center celebration, persuasion, or release without inventing speeches, lovers, bargains, or outcomes beyond the supplied facts

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: communal and vivid, ceremony turning belief into shared action

tones.li: warm and public, people briefly gathered around one purpose
festive but observant, joy and politics sharing the same space

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: VIE ritual aftereffects
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the after-image of a rite or festival - write glory, awkwardness, shame, dread, or relief settling into one honest private feeling

instructions.li: ritual after-image - keep the public ceremony in the background and the feeling it left in the foreground
ritual after-image - write one residue that stayed: pride, embarrassment, freedom, fear, tenderness, or regret

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: reflective, with the ceremony's emotional residue still present

tones.li: quiet and inward, the crowd gone but the feeling remaining
spent and honest, public ritual becoming private memory

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Interrogations
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: pressure applied across a table - write what the pawn did to another person or held out against, with power, guilt, fear, or cold procedure but no invented confession

instructions.li: an interrogation - center who controlled the room, what pressure felt like, and what the supplied result changed
an interrogation - write the moral distance or closeness between interrogator and prisoner; quote nothing not present in the rendered log
an interrogation - keep intimidation, refusal, and success distinct while inventing neither a crime nor the information sought

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: controlled and coercive, every silence carrying pressure

tones.li: cold and procedural, power made ordinary
tense and exposed, resistance measured in pauses
